reasoning: There was no recession in 2023 or through the first three quarters of 2024, with US GDP growing steadily. The NBER defines a recession as a significant decline in economic activity. For a recession to have started in 2024, it would have to begin in late Q4 2024. Even if this occurred, the NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee typically takes 12-18 months to officially declare the start of a recession. Thus, for a Q4 2024 recession, an announcement by the end of 2025 is highly improbable. Given the strong economic data of 2023-2024, the likelihood of an NBER declaration of a 2023/2024 recession by the end of 2025 is exceptionally low.
